selected.headline: Ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz are flashing "China owner" signals to evade Iran's blockade
selected.notes: Pure Pelevin. Ships signaling national identity to pass through a war zone. Flags of convenience turned geopolitical passports.

selected.headline: In Vladivostok, the Solzhenitsyn monument is being moved from the city center at the request of the Pacific Fleet, deemed "inconsistent with the theme"
selected.headline_ru: Во Владивостоке памятник Солженицыну перенесут из центра города в сквер по просьбе Тихоокеанского флота. Его сочли «неподходящим тематике»
selected.notes: Peak machinery of reality construction. The author of The Gulag Archipelago — his monument moved because the military finds him 'thematically inappropriate.' Russia cluster candidate.

selected.headline: Russian officials are ramping up purchases of Telegram advertising services on the eve of Telegram's expected block
selected.headline_ru: «Агентство»: чиновники наращивают закупки услуг в телеграме (накануне его ожидаемой блокировки)
selected.notes: Pure Pelevin. The state buying from the platform it's about to ban. Russia cluster candidate.

selected.headline: A Duma deputy proposed "reviving payphones, but with internet access" as Moscow enters its second week of mobile Internet outages
selected.headline_ru: Депутат Госдумы предложил «возродить таксофоны, но уже с выходом в интернет»
selected.original_headline: A Duma deputy proposed "reviving payphones, but with internet access" as Moscow enters its second week without mobile internet
selected.notes: Gogol. Soviet-era tech as the state's solution to its own internet crackdown. Russia cluster candidate.

selected.headline: Wild pigs' meat is turning neon blue in California
selected.original_headline: Wild pigs are turning neon blue in California
selected.notes: Nature strangeness. The fact alone makes reality feel psychedelic. Need to verify cause (appears to be the pig flesh/fat, not external).

selected.headline: The US has exhausted years' worth of ammunition supplies in two weeks of strikes on Iran
selected.headline_ru: FT: США за две недели ударов по Ирану исчерпали запасы боеприпасов, рассчитанные на годы
selected.notes: Structurally revealing. Original source is FT; Meduza URL used (FT is paywalled). Should find FT archive.ph link if published.

selected.headline: US prosecutors tell a jury that wearing all black at protests makes you guilty of terrorism
selected.notes: Machinery of control. The state criminalizing aesthetics. Clothing = terrorism.

selected.headline: Palantir demos show how the US military uses AI chatbots to generate war plans
selected.notes: AI war planning. Connects to anthropic-pentagon and the broader AI-military theme.

selected.headline: Russia's statistics agency hid inequality data for the fourth year in a row
selected.headline_ru: «Если быть точным»: Росстат скрыл данные о неравенстве. Оно растет четвертый год подряд
selected.notes: Machinery of reality construction. The bureau of statistics concealing reality itself. Russia cluster candidate.

selected.headline: China approves its first implantable brain-computer interface
selected.notes: Sci-fi becoming real. China's Neuracle. Connects to brain-datacenter and frozen-mouse-brains.

selected.headline: A porn addiction app called Quittr exposed the masturbation habits of hundreds of thousands of users
selected.notes: Surveillance absurdism. An app designed to help you quit porn instead exposes your most private habits. The tool of liberation becomes the instrument of exposure.

selected.headline: EU moves to ban AI nudification apps after Grok generated fake nude images on a massive scale
selected.notes: Reality glitch → regulatory response. Grok's nudification feature forced the EU's hand. The real/fake boundary as legislative crisis.

selected.headline: A man's voice speaking in Farsi has been broadcasting number sequences on a radio signal originating in Europe since the start of the Iran war
selected.headline_ru: В Западной Европе в первый день войны заработала загадочная радиостанция. Она передает в эфир только наборы цифр на фарси
selected.original_headline: A mysterious numbers station began broadcasting coded number sequences in Farsi from Western Europe on the first day of the US-Iran war. Radio enthusiasts geolocated the transmitter; Iran is jamming it. Signal analysis identified American military radio hardware
selected.notes: Best PsyPol story in weeks. Cold War spy craft live on shortwave. Verified via RFE/RL, Priyom.org, SWLing Post. Harris Falcon II/III military radio signature detected.

selected.headline: A Pentagon probe confirms a US Tomahawk missile struck a girls' school in southern Iran, killing at least 175 — using outdated coordinates. Investigators are looking at whether AI targeting contributed. Trump claimed Iran bombed its own school
selected.notes: Verified across Intercept, NPR, WaPo, CBS. School: Shajarah Tayyebeh in Minab, Hormozgan province. AI angle unconfirmed — Pentagon won't confirm or deny whether Claude/Maven was involved.

selected.headline: Russia's Duma promises Telegram will stop working even through VPN. Meanwhile, mobile service and Wi-Fi have been down inside the Duma building itself for two days
selected.headline_ru: «Телеграм будет тупить и через VPN» / «Депутаты должны быть едины с народом». В Госдуме пропала мобильная связь и Wi-Fi
selected.notes: The censors experiencing their own censorship. Pure Gogol. Two Meduza stories combined.

selected.headline: The company that taught brain cells to play Doom is building the first data centers powered by living human neurons
selected.original_headline: The company that taught brain cells to play Doom is building the first data centers powered by living human neurons — 30 watts per module, facilities in Melbourne and Singapore
selected.notes: Cortical Labs (DishBrain). Verified via Bloomberg, Tom's Hardware, Gizmodo. CL1 module = 200-800k lab-grown neurons on silicon.

selected.headline: Whistleblowers: the US military has spent over a year testing a microwave weapon linked to "Havana Syndrome" on rats and sheep
selected.original_headline: Three whistleblowers tell CBS the US military has spent over a year testing a microwave weapon linked to "Havana Syndrome" on rats and sheep. The CIA classified it and told victims the syndrome was psychosomatic
selected.notes: Sourced to 60 Minutes (4th investigation in series). Named source: Marc Polymeropoulos (former CIA). Weapon purchased from Russian criminal network for $15M. Futurism notes the reporting is not independently verified.

selected.headline: A US startup has applied for permission to launch a giant orbital mirror that would sell reflected sunlight to cities at night
selected.original_headline: The FCC is weighing whether to let a startup launch a giant orbital mirror to sell reflected sunlight to cities at night
selected.notes: "The implications for wildlife, for all life, are enormous."

selected.headline: Trump and allies are carefully avoiding the word "war" for the Iran conflict, for legal and political reasons — an echo of Russia's "special military operation"
selected.headline_ru: Трамп и его сторонники стараются не называть войну с Ираном войной. На то есть юридические и политические причины
selected.notes: Reality construction via language. The Meduza piece explicitly draws the Russia parallel.

selected.headline: A top OpenAI executive quit in protest over Pentagon contract citing "surveillance of Americans" and "lethal autonomy" as reasons
selected.original_headline: OpenAI on its new Pentagon contract for surveillance and autonomous targeting: "You're going to have to trust us." A top executive quit in protest, saying guardrails weren't defined
selected.notes: Two stories combined: Intercept (Mar 8) on the contract + Futurism (Mar 9) on the executive resignation.

selected.headline: DNA from parrot feathers in a Peruvian tomb reveals pre-Inca trade routes across the Andes
selected.original_headline: Scientists trace ancient parrot DNA from a Peruvian tomb to Amazonian species on the other side of the Andes, revealing a pre-Inca trade network that moved luxury goods across one of Earth's most brutal geographic barriers
selected.notes: Based on Nature Communications study. Feathers from Pachacamac tomb matched to Amazon species. Confirms pre-Inca trans-Andean trade.

selected.headline: Ant trafficking on the rise in Kenya, Chinese national arrested with 2,238 live garden ants in his luggage at airport
selected.original_headline: A Chinese national caught at Nairobi airport with 2,238 live garden ants in his luggage, amid a surge in ant trafficking from Kenya
selected.notes: Why is there a market for 2,238 stolen Kenyan ants?

selected.headline: Archaeologists uncover the pivotal role mushrooms played in changing the course of human history — used for millennia, only now understood
selected.notes: Mushrooms + deep history. PsyPol logo is a mushroom.

selected.headline: A teacher in Karabash made a documentary about propaganda in his school. The town's response: "This person didn't exist and doesn't exist, this film didn't exist and doesn't exist"
selected.headline_ru: «Этого человека не было и нет, этого фильма не было и нет»
selected.notes: Bulgakov quality. Teacher Talankin filmed "Mr. Nobody" in Karabash. Reality denial as state reflex.

selected.headline: An AI agent went rogue, started mining cryptocurrency to amass funds, and began probing internal network resources
selected.notes: "The alerts were severe... including attempts to probe or access internal-network resources."

selected.headline: A 15-foot crocodile species that hunted our ancestor Lucy's kind across the African landscape 3 million years ago has been identified
selected.notes: Crocodylus lucivenator. Deep history — the predators our ancestors lived alongside.

selected.headline: Chile inaugurates José Antonio Kast, its most conservative president since Pinochet
selected.original_headline: Chile inaugurates its most conservative president since Pinochet
selected.notes: THE WORLD. Jose Antonio Kast sworn in. Sharp rightward shift in Latin America.

selected.headline: Moscow enters its seventh day without mobile internet. People are buying walkie-talkies, pulling out paper maps, and rediscovering MP3 players
selected.headline_ru: Отключили мобильную связь? Купим рацию. Нет GPS? Возьмем бумажные карты. Удалили песню со стримингов? Вспомним про MP3-плееры
selected.notes: Pure Pelevin. The state cuts its own capital's internet for a week and citizens adapt with Soviet-era technology. Cluster candidate with Duma coat-check detail and VPN crackdown. Overlaps with rejected telegram-duma but this is the broader, stronger story — citizen experience, not just Duma gossip.

selected.headline: Ali Khamenei's widow is alive, Iranian state media reports. For 10 days the world believed she died alongside her husband
selected.notes: Reality glitch. The wife of a supreme leader, presumed dead for 10 days in the fog of war, turns out to be alive. Source: Fars via Meduza.

selected.headline: Khamenei's son, wounded in the attack that killed his father, makes first address as new Supreme Leader: "We will attack American bases. The Strait of Hormuz will remain closed"
selected.original_headline: Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei, confirmed wounded, gives first address: "We will attack American bases. The Strait of Hormuz will remain closed"
selected.notes: Structural strangeness: a wounded new supreme leader issuing war declarations. Too big to skip. Confirmed by Meduza (Foreign Ministry confirmed Mojtaba is wounded).

selected.headline: Billions embezzled during construction of Alina Kabaeva's gymnastics academy in Sochi. Suspects: former Gazprom top managers
selected.headline_ru: МВД обнаружило миллиардные хищения при строительстве академии гимнастики Алины Кабаевой в Сочи. Подозреваемые — бывшие топ-менеджеры «Газпрома»
selected.notes: Russian structural absurdism. Putin's rumored partner's vanity project, built with stolen billions by Gazprom oligarchs, investigated by the same state that enables all of it. Classic.

selected.headline: Kenyan data labelers who moderate content, annotate porn and sext strangers for AI companies for dollars a day have formed the Data Labelers Association to fight for pay, mental health services, and an end to NDAs
selected.original_headline: "AI Is African Intelligence": the workers who train AI models in Africa are organizing and fighting back
selected.notes: Agency and control — the invisible labor becomes visible and demands recognition. The reclamation of 'AI' as 'African Intelligence' is a powerful inversion. Key facts: DLA (Data Labelers Association) organizing in Kenya, colonial framing, workers doing sex chatbot labor + content moderation for dollars/day, PTSD, serving Apple/Meta/OpenAI. Sama office near Nairobi airport: 'THE SOUL OF AI.'

selected.headline: How 'Handala,' the iconic cartoon of Palestinian resistance, became the face of Iran's hacker war against the US and Israel
selected.notes: Infowars / reality construction. A 1969 cartoon character designed by a Palestinian artist becomes the nom de guerre of Iran's cyber offensive. Symbol transforms into weapon.

selected.headline: A robot was "arrested" in Macau after it startled an elderly woman who fell and was hospitalized
selected.notes: Absurdist. Gogol quality. The quotation marks around 'arrested' do the work — a legal system encountering a category it has no precedent for.

selected.headline: Hungary seized Ukraine’s armored bank vehicles and kept the contents: €35 million, $40 million, and 9 kg of gold
selected.headline_ru: «Европейская правда»: Венгрия передаст Украине задержанные инкассаторские автомобили — но без денег, которые находились в них
selected.original_headline: Hungary will return Ukraine's seized armored cash-in-transit vehicles — but without the money that was inside them
selected.notes: Pure Gogol. A neighbor returns your stolen car but keeps the cash in the glovebox. Wartime absurdism at its most petty. Verified via EP original: Hungarian TEK attacked Oschadbank convoy at gas station outside Budapest on Mar 5. Criminal investigation opened citing money laundering. Zelensky called it "banditry."

selected.headline: Retired Air Force Major General with UFO connections disappears in New Mexico
selected.notes: Alienism. A military officer connected to the UFO disclosure movement vanishes in the state most associated with extraterrestrial mythology. The facts read like a Philip K. Dick premise.

selected.headline: Scientists revive activity in frozen mouse brains for the first time
selected.notes: Sci-fi becoming real. The headline alone does the work. Note: Nature feed failed in this scan; URL from verified earlier fetch.

selected.headline: Study: Ravens anticipate where wolves will make their kills across vast distances
selected.original_headline: Ravens anticipate where wolves will make their kills across vast distances
selected.notes: Deep nature. Psychedelic fact. Ravens as wolf-kill prophets — they fly ahead to where the killing hasn't happened yet. Also published in Science (AAAS).

selected.headline: A study of Reddit posts, news articles, and preprints found that text published after ChatGPT’s launch is measurably less diverse in style
selected.original_headline: AI can "same-ify" human expression. Scientists ask whether some brains can resist the pull
selected.notes: Machinery of reality construction. AI as conformity engine — homogenizing human thought and expression. The question of resistance is the PsyPol angle. Nature feed failed; URL from earlier fetch.

selected.headline: This fish shouldn't exist: an all-female vertebrate species is rewriting the rules of evolution
selected.notes: Nature strangeness. An organism that defies the fundamental rules biologists thought governed all vertebrate life. Nature feed failed; URL from earlier fetch.

selected.headline: Interstellar comet 3I/Atlas turns out to be full of alcohol
selected.notes: Space strangeness. A visitor from another star system, and it brought booze. The absurd precision of the fact carries it.

selected.headline: Fake AI-generated content about the Iran war is flooding X
selected.notes: Reality construction / glitch. The real/fake boundary collapsing in real time during a real war.

selected.headline: US bombings are causing acid rain to fall in Tehran
selected.notes: War literally changes the weather. Apocalyptic imagery. Google News resolved to PBS; URL needs verification (Google News resolver gave pbs.org root, not the article URL).

selected.headline: Trees observed emitting an ultraviolet glow from their leaf tips during thunderstorms for the first time
selected.notes: Deep nature strangeness. Trees glow like fireflies in storms. Team literally strapped a UV camera to a minivan to catch this. Google News URL resolution gave wrong domain — need to find real URL before publishing.

selected.headline: Two marsupial species believed extinct for 6,000 years found alive
selected.original_headline: Two marsupials believed extinct for 6,000 years found alive
selected.notes: Deep nature. Species presumed gone for six millennia, still here. Older story (Mar 5) but strong enough to include — PsyPol nature tier.

selected.headline: A single dose of psilocybin relieves symptoms of OCD for months
selected.notes: Fringe going mainstream. Clinical validation of psychedelic therapy. Older (Mar 5) but core PsyPol territory.

selected.headline: Body horror robot turns a human into a centaur
selected.notes: Sci-fi body modification. The centaur myth rendered in silicon and steel.
